name: Factoring advance on hold (money + urgency)
subject: Factoring advance on hold — action needed to release $18,420.55
trucking_context: Small carriers run cash-tight on factored advances; an unexpected hold that threatens Friday payroll is an emotionally loaded pretext that lookalike portals exploit against accounting teams.
indicators: Sender domain tafs-carrier-portal.co is not tafs.com
Real factoring companies have direct AR contacts on file — funding holds are usually phoned, not emailed cold
Specific dollar amount and load numbers add false credibility but are easy to fabricate
'Phone disputes are queued behind portal submissions' is engineered to defeat out-of-band verification
Friday payroll pressure is a textbook urgency lure
